battery.action.charge.context: Action for a home battery storage system. The battery is being charged with electricity. NOT a financial charge or fee.

battery.action.charge_from_grid.context: The home battery is being forcibly charged using electricity from the power grid, typically during cheap price hours. NOT a fee.

battery.action.discharge.context: The home battery is releasing stored electricity to power the house. NOT a financial discharge.

battery.action.discharge_to_grid.context: The home battery is sending electricity back to the power grid.

battery.action.idle.context: The battery is doing nothing — neither charging nor discharging.

battery.action.self_consume.context: Solar electricity is being used directly by the household without going through the battery or grid.

battery.status.soc.context: State of Charge — the percentage of energy currently stored in the battery (0-100%).

battery.info.efficiency.context: The percentage of energy retained during a full charge and discharge cycle. Energy is lost as heat. NOT a financial efficiency.

price.incl_tax.context: Electricity or gas price including government taxes (VAT/BTW). NOT related to battery charging.

price.excl_tax.context: Electricity or gas price excluding government taxes. NOT related to battery.

optimizer.reason.cheap_price.context: The optimizer chose to charge the battery because the electricity price is low right now.

optimizer.reason.high_price.context: The optimizer chose to discharge the battery because the electricity price is high right now.

optimizer.reason.solar_surplus.context: The solar panels are producing more electricity than the house is consuming, so the surplus is stored in the battery.

optimizer.reason.min_soc.context: The battery has reached its minimum allowed state of charge and cannot discharge further.

consumption.grid_import.context: Electricity drawn from the public power grid into the home.

consumption.grid_export.context: Electricity sent back from the home to the public power grid (also called feed-in or back-feeding).

report.daily.charged_from_grid.context: Amount of electricity (kWh) that was used to charge the home battery from the power grid today. NOT a financial charge.

weather.irradiance.context: Solar irradiance — the amount of solar energy hitting a surface, measured in Watts per square metre (W/m²). Used to estimate solar panel output.

system.min_discharge_price.context: The minimum electricity price (€/kWh) at which the optimizer will allow the battery to discharge. Below this price it is not worth discharging.

system.max_charge_price.context: The maximum electricity price (€/kWh) at which the optimizer will allow charging the battery from the grid. Above this price it is too expensive to charge.
